source: NvChad - Install NvChad: the files under ~/.config/nvim and the default key mappings

questions.q: Which file initializes and coordinates the whole configuration?
questions.why: init.lua is the starting file that loads everything else.

options: The installed plugins and their commit state, for syncing across machines
questions.why: It records each plugin's status so installs can be replicated.

questions.q: Which file is used to customize the editor's appearance (UI)?
questions.why: chadrc.lua shares syntax with the plugin's nvconfig.lua for UI settings.

questions.q: Where should your own (personal) plugins go?
questions.why: Personal plugins live in lua/plugins; their extra configs in lua/configs.
configs holds plugin configuration such as conform.lua.

questions.q: In mappings, which single-letter reference means Ctrl?
questions.why: C = Ctrl, A = Alt, S = Shift, leader = Space.

questions.q: Which default mappings open nvimtree and a floating terminal?
options: <C-n> opens nvimtree; <A-i> opens a floating terminal
questions.why: Ctrl+n toggles nvimtree; Alt+i opens a floating terminal.

questions.q: What is the default insert-mode escape mapping?
